summary: Danish school mock elections in 2024 showed a surprising rightward shift among young voters, with the center-right Liberal Alliance winning a third of votes. DHH connects this to the post-Greta Thunberg generation growing up under predictions of imminent climate catastrophe that never materialized. He argues this has bred healthy skepticism toward expert predictions generally. Rather than analyzing the full phenomenon, he focuses on the idea that each generation rejects the apocalyptic narratives of its predecessors.
key_points: Danish youth mock elections showed a startling shift toward center-right politics, challenging assumptions that young people are inherently left-leaning
The current cohort of young voters grew up during the Greta Thunberg era but never saw the predicted climate catastrophe materialize, potentially dulling climate alarmism as a motivating issue
Repeated failures of expert predictions across climate, economics, and politics breed healthy skepticism in those who live through enough missed forecasts
Each generation may naturally reject the apocalyptic narratives inherited from the previous one
money_quotes: The results for 2024 were a startling refutation of the idea that young people must be inherently left-leaning.
The ones who failed to see the world come to an end in short order, as at times breathlessly predicted, and, perhaps as a result, no longer list climate alarmism as a central concern.
The weather, the money, and the people have a funny way of proving experts wrong time and again.
Just when they think they have it all figured out, the story takes an unexpected turn.
You really don't have to live through too many of such turns before you start developing a healthy skepticism for the people who are so sure about what's going to happen next.
Having lived their entire childhood under a story of imminent climate calamity, they've become young adults without seeing that doom's day come to their calendar.
Maybe no generation wants to inherit the favorite apocalypse of the one that went before it.
key_insight: Generations raised under apocalyptic predictions that fail to materialize develop a natural skepticism toward those narratives and the experts who promoted them.
